Source: Dia Security
URL: https://diabrowser.com/security
Fetched: 2026-03-22

# Dia Browser Security

## Bug Bounty
Vulnerability disclosure via HackerOne (hackerone.com/bcny)

## Security Architecture
- Annual full-scope security assessments: code reviews, architecture analysis, exploit testing
- Point-in-time assessments for high-risk features
- Built on Chromium (same engine as Chrome and Edge) with aggressive upstream security patch schedule
- Weekly updates (Thursdays); critical vulnerabilities patched within 48 hours of Google releases

## Disabled Chromium Features
- Google Accounts Integration (GAIA) disabled
- Google metrics (UMA) reporting disabled
- Settings upload after profile reset disabled
- Reporting Observers and Reporting API disabled
- Network logging to file disabled

## Infrastructure
- Role-restricted production access with regular logging reviews
- Data encrypted at rest and in transit
- Minimal personal data collection with routine audits

## Data Handling & Privacy
- Conversations, history, bookmarks stored locally encrypted on device
- Minimum necessary data briefly sent to AI partners
- Contractual restrictions: model providers restricted from training on or retaining user data
- Opt-in improvement data: de-identified, retained max 30 days
- Incognito mode stores nothing
- AI data retained only for security/compliance (content moderation, suspected misuse); access-controlled, deleted when purpose expires, never used for training

## AI Safety Controls / Prompt Injection Safeguards
- Blocks AI-generated URLs
- Requires user approval for tool access
- Prevents data insertion into third-party sites without confirmation
- Keeps irreversible actions in draft status
- Restricts agent navigation between websites
- URL filtering for language models
- Eight specific layered controls to limit injection risks
- Note: some attacks can still cause unexpected style or tone shifts without compromising system security

Contact: help@diabrowser.com
